name: Self-Determination Theory (SDT)
overview: Self-Determination Theory (SDT) is a macro-theory of human motivation developed by Edward L. Deci and Richard M. Ryan starting in the 1970s at the University of Rochester. It posits three innate basic psychological needs (BPN) - Autonomy, Competence, and Relatedness - whose satisfaction produces intrinsic motivation, persistence, and well-being, while their frustration produces amotivation, ill-being, and burnout. SDT also formalizes the Overjustification Effect: salient extrinsic rewards (badges, points, money) can crowd out intrinsic motivation by shifting the perceived locus of causality from internal to external, especially when rewards feel controlling rather than informational.
originator_or_company: Edward L. Deci (1942-2026) and Richard M. Ryan, Department of Clinical and Social Sciences in Psychology, University of Rochester (Human Motivation Program founded 1981). Allied researchers extending SDT into digital products: Scott Rigby (Immersyve, co-author of 'Glued to Games' with Ryan, 2011), Maarten Vansteenkiste (Ghent), Joshua Howard (Monash), Frederic Guay (Laval). Yu-kai Chou's Octalysis framework explicitly cites SDT as one of its underpinnings.
key_resources: [Ryan & Deci (2000) - Self-Determination Theory and the Facilitation of Intrinsic Motivation, Social Development, and Well-Being - American Psychologist](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/SDT/documents/2000_RyanDeci_SDT.pdf)

[Ryan & Deci (2020) - Intrinsic and Extrinsic Motivation from a Self-Determination Theory Perspective - Contemporary Educational Psychology 61](https://stial.ie/resources/Ryan%20and%20Deci%202020%20self%20determination%20theory.pdf)
[Deci (1971) - Effects of externally mediated rewards on intrinsic motivation - JPSP](https://www.selfdeterminationtheory.org/SDT/documents/1971_Deci.pdf)

[Howard, Bureau, Guay, Chong & Ryan (2021) - Student Motivation and Associated Outcomes: A Meta-Analysis - Perspectives on Psychological Science](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/2021_HowardBureauGuayChongRyan_Student-Motivation_PrePrint.pdf)

[Bureau, Howard, Chong & Guay (2022) - Pathways to Student Motivation: A Meta-Analysis (144 studies, 79,000+ students) - Review of Educational Research](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/2021_PathwaystoStudentMotiv_BureaHowardEtal.pdf)
[Rigby & Ryan (2011) - Glued to Games: How Video Games Draw Us In and Hold Us Spellbound](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/glued-games-video-games-draw-us-hold-us-spellbound/)
[selfdeterminationtheory.org - Official theory hub maintained by Ryan & colleagues](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/theory/)
[NN/g - Autonomy, Relatedness, and Competence in UX Design (Sarah Gibbons)](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/autonomy-relatedness-competence/)

[Yu-kai Chou - Self-Determination Theory Guide to Ryan and Deci's Motivation Framework](https://yukaichou.com/gamification-analysis/self-determination-theory-guide-to-ryan-and-decis-motivation-framework/)
[APA - The Intrinsic Motivation of Richard Ryan and Edward Deci](https://www.apa.org/members/content/intrinsic-motivation)
core_mechanics: SDT is composed of six mini-theories. (1) COGNITIVE EVALUATION THEORY (CET): explains how external events (rewards, deadlines, surveillance, feedback) affect intrinsic motivation by changing perceived autonomy and competence. Tangible expected rewards contingent on engagement undermine intrinsic motivation; informational positive feedback enhances it. (2) ORGANISMIC INTEGRATION THEORY (OIT): defines a continuum of extrinsic motivation - external regulation -> introjected regulation -> identified regulation -> integrated regulation -> intrinsic motivation. The further right on the continuum, the more autonomous and the better the outcomes. (3) CAUSALITY ORIENTATIONS THEORY (COT): individual differences in autonomy vs control vs impersonal orientations. (4) BASIC PSYCHOLOGICAL NEEDS THEORY (BPNT): Autonomy = volition and self-endorsement of action (NOT independence/individualism); Competence = experience of mastery and effective interaction with environment; Relatedness = feeling cared for and connected. All three are necessary, not substitutable. (5) GOAL CONTENTS THEORY (GCT): intrinsic aspirations (growth, relationships, community) vs extrinsic aspirations (wealth, fame, image) - intrinsic aspirations correlate with well-being. (6) RELATIONSHIPS MOTIVATION THEORY (RMT): high-quality relationships satisfy relatedness AND autonomy. APPLIED PRINCIPLES FOR DESIGN: (a) Provide MEANINGFUL CHOICE (autonomy) - not paradox-of-choice overload, but volitional pacing/path/customization. (b) Provide OPTIMAL CHALLENGE (competence) with clear, INFORMATIONAL feedback (NOT controlling 'you must'). (c) Provide social PRESENCE/CARE (relatedness) without forced ranking. (d) Frame rewards as INFORMATIONAL ('you mastered X') not CONTROLLING ('do X to earn Y'). (e) Use UNEXPECTED, task-noncontingent rewards - they do not undermine intrinsic motivation, while expected/contingent rewards do.
designer_or_researcher_voice: Edward Deci (Rochester video, 2011): 'Most psychologists don't think about people as living entities. They think about them as machines.' This frames Deci's lifelong critique of behaviorist Skinner-box reinforcement applied to humans.

Ryan & Deci (2000, American Psychologist): 'Intrinsic motivation is the inherent tendency to seek out novelty and challenges, to extend and exercise one's capacities, to explore, and to learn. ... social-contextual events (e.g., feedback, communications, rewards) that conduce toward feelings of competence during action can enhance intrinsic motivation for that action.'

Ryan & Deci (2020, Contemporary Educational Psychology): 'Feedback can have informational significance if it is efficacy-relevant - helping the person improve - which tends to enhance intrinsic motivation, in contrast to feedback with controlling significance experienced as pressure toward specific behaviors.' This is the design rule for shikaq's correctness feedback copy.

Scott Rigby (Immersyve, co-author Glued to Games, 2011): 'The deepest engagement in games comes not from rewards or graphics, but from satisfying the same psychological needs that drive every human pursuit - the need to feel competent, to act with volition, and to feel meaningfully connected to others.' [paraphrased synthesis from Glued to Games]

Richard Ryan (APA Profile): 'When you reward people for doing something they already enjoy, you transform play into work.' This is the canonical statement of the Overjustification Effect that anchors anti-pattern review for any badge/point system.
metrics_or_evidence: Howard, Bureau, Guay, Chong & Ryan (2021) meta-analysis (344 samples, 223,209 participants) found intrinsic motivation strongly predicts well-being (rho ~ .51) and academic achievement (rho ~ .27); identified regulation predicts persistence (rho ~ .47); external regulation negatively relates to well-being (rho ~ -.14). Bureau et al. (2022) meta-analysis (144 studies, 79,000+ students) found COMPETENCE is the strongest BPN predictor of self-determined motivation (rho ~ .58 with intrinsic motivation, rho = -.38 with amotivation), followed by autonomy (rho ~ .53 with identified regulation) and relatedness (rho ~ .23 with introjected). Teacher autonomy support outpredicts parental autonomy support for school motivation. Wang et al. (2024) systematic review of SDT-based educational interventions found small-to-moderate positive effects on intrinsic motivation, engagement, and achievement (g ~ 0.30-0.45). Deci, Koestner & Ryan (1999) meta-analysis (128 studies) on rewards: tangible expected rewards contingent on task engagement reduced free-choice intrinsic motivation by ~ d = -0.36; verbal positive feedback (informational) ENHANCED intrinsic motivation by d ~ +0.31. Sources: selfdeterminationtheory.org, PubMed PMC8935530, Sage Perspectives 2021.
criticisms: (1) WESTERN INDIVIDUALIST BIAS: Iyengar & Lepper (1999) showed Asian American children persisted longer when their MOTHERS chose anagram tasks (vs Euro-Americans who persisted with self-chosen tasks), challenging the universality of personal-choice autonomy. Counter: Chirkov, Ryan, Kim & Kaplan (2003) reframe autonomy as VOLITIONAL ENDORSEMENT (not independence) and find SDT replicates cross-culturally; Korean, Japanese, and Chinese samples show same BPN -> well-being links. (2) MOTIVATION CONTINUUM OPERATIONALIZATION: differentiating introjected vs identified regulation is methodologically fragile; Relative Autonomy Index has been criticized for psychometric weakness (Howard et al., 2017). (3) DOMAIN OF UNDERMINING EFFECT IS NARROWER THAN POPULAR ACCOUNTS: Cameron & Pierce (1994) re-analyzed reward studies and argued the undermining effect is small and limited; Deci, Koestner & Ryan (1999, 2001) re-confirmed it but agreed it depends on reward salience and contingency. (4) OVER-REACH: SDT is sometimes invoked as universal explanation for everything from politics to weight loss; specificity of effect is weaker for habit/automaticity-driven behaviors than for novel learning. (5) JAPAN/EAST ASIA: longitudinal Japanese junior-high data (Hayamizu, Otoshi - ScienceDirect 2017) shows autonomous motivation DECREASES while controlled motivation INCREASES through schooling, suggesting SDT-prescribed need support is rare in exam-oriented Japanese pedagogy.
failure_cases: (1) Pure-token classroom economies (sticker charts, all-extrinsic rubrics) repeatedly show short-term compliance lift then collapse when tokens stop - the canonical failure mode predicted by CET. (2) Corporate forced-ranking systems (GE Stack Ranking, Microsoft pre-2013) explicitly thwart relatedness (peer-against-peer) and autonomy (mandatory curve), and were retired after burnout/turnover damage. (3) Khan Academy's heavy badge layer in early 2010s drew SDT critique (Mitra, others) for risking overjustification on math practice; Khan later softened badge prominence in favor of mastery-progress visualizations (informational feedback). (4) Duolingo's Hearts system is the textbook anti-SDT case: gates COMPETENCE practice behind paywall, removes AUTONOMY of free retry, induces controlled motivation to avoid loss - widely flagged in App Store reviews and education-research critiques. (5) Workplace OKR systems that bonus-link KRs frequently surface the undermining effect: employees game targets at the cost of intrinsic engagement (cf. Wells Fargo cross-selling scandal as extreme failure mode of controlling extrinsic incentives).
antipatterns: (1) EXPECTED CONTINGENT REWARDS on intrinsically interesting tasks - canonical Overjustification trigger. Do NOT promise 'finish 5 lessons and get 100 gems'; the reward becomes the locus of causality and learning collapses when it stops. (2) CONTROLLING LANGUAGE in copy ('you MUST', 'you HAVE TO', 'don't lose your streak!') - thwarts autonomy. SDT-aligned copy uses 'you can', 'when you're ready', 'you chose this path'. (3) FORCED PUBLIC RANKING (leaderboards everyone enters) thwarts relatedness for non-Achievers and creates introjected motivation. Make leaderboards opt-in. (4) SURVEILLANCE-FRAMED FEEDBACK ('we noticed you haven't studied in 3 days') triggers controlling perception; reframe as informational ('your last session was 3 days ago - want to pick up where you left off?'). (5) CHOICE OVERLOAD masquerading as autonomy (50 settings on first launch) - autonomy is volitional endorsement, NOT decision burden. (6) HEARTS/LIVES PAYWALLS that gate practice - simultaneously thwarts competence (cannot practice to mastery) and autonomy (forced to wait or pay). (7) GUILT-INDUCING NOTIFICATIONS (Duolingo evil-owl style) - induce introjected regulation, short-term compliance, long-term churn. (8) ZERO-RELATEDNESS solo apps for socially-motivated personas - shikaq must offer at minimum lightweight social presence (study-buddy, anonymized cohort) for the Relatedness need.
applicability_to_shikaq: SDT is the THEORETICAL BACKBONE that should govern every shikaq design decision; it is more important than HEXAD or Hooked because it gives us the ethical guardrails. Translation for the 20s-female cafe-iPad-Apple-HIG persona: (A) AUTONOMY: (a1) Let users CHOOSE their daily goal (3 / 10 / 20 questions) and edit it any day - NOT impose. (a2) Let users choose their certification path order (IT Pass -> FE, or Boki first) and switch freely. (a3) On Today screen, show 'recommended next' but never force - persona resists controlling pedagogy. (a4) Notification copy: 'When you have a moment, today's review is ready' (volitional) NOT 'You missed yesterday!' (controlling). (a5) Settings should expose pacing controls (Duolingo's Coach goal but framed as self-care, not commitment). (B) COMPETENCE: (b1) Mastery progress visualization (Apple Fitness ring metaphor, but framed as mastery percentage by topic, NOT raw XP). (b2) INFORMATIONAL feedback on wrong answers - explain WHY, link to source lesson - never just 'X wrong'. (b3) Difficulty scaling via FSRS-like adaptive scheduling so most attempts land in the optimal challenge zone. (b4) Celebrate genuine milestones (first full topic mastered, first mock exam passed) with subdued Apple-style haptic+symbol, NOT confetti chests. (C) RELATEDNESS: (c1) Optional anonymized study-buddy / cohort for SPI/boki (peer presence without competition). (c2) Family Plan / Apple shareplay-style 'study together' room for cafe-with-friend usage. (c3) Admin/AI feedback voice should be CARING, second-person, neither aggressive nor saccharine - the Apple Intelligence tone. (D) AVOID OVERJUSTIFICATION: do NOT promise gems/badges for completing lessons. If shikaq adds rewards, make them UNEXPECTED, task-noncontingent, INFORMATIONAL ('you cleared 3 sections this week - here's a personalized weak-point summary'), not 'do X to earn Y'. (E) HEXAD MAPPING: SDT directly underwrites Free Spirit (autonomy), Achiever (competence), Socializer + Philanthropist (relatedness). Player and Disruptor types are explicitly DE-PRIORITIZED because their reward mechanics conflict with SDT and shikaq's ethics stance. (F) PRICING: month-to-month 980 yen with no streak-loss penalty; pause/resume to support autonomous self-pacing.
phase_assignment: Phase 1 (3 weeks): bake SDT principles into copywriting, notification system, and feedback design from day one - this is a guardrail, not a feature. Specifically: write Today/Practice/Summary copy in autonomy-supportive voice; ensure correctness feedback is informational (explain why) not controlling. Phase 2 (4-6 weeks): ship customizable daily goal, opt-in cohort/study-buddy (relatedness), mastery-percentage visualization (competence). Phase 3 (6-8 weeks): adaptive challenge scaling, integrated regulation support (long-term aspiration linkage: 'why are you preparing for FE?' -> persistent reminder of user's own answer), unexpected non-contingent rewards. Rationale: SDT compliance is cross-cutting and CHEAPER to enforce as a design principle from start than to retrofit.
integration_points: Cross-cutting design principle, not a single component. Touch points: (1) Sidebar - daily goal indicator must be user-set, editable; no streak-shame badge. (2) TodayContent - recommended next is suggestion, not mandate; copy uses 'when you're ready'. (3) Practice (questions) - feedback panel after each answer must include WHY (informational), link to source lesson, never plain 'wrong'. (4) Summary - mastery rings by topic + qualitative weakness explanation, NOT raw leaderboard. (5) AIQA - tone calibrated to caring/informational. (6) profiles table - add columns: timezone, daily_goal_questions (default 5, user-editable), notification_tone ('gentle' / 'minimal'), aspiration_text (free-form 'why are you studying?'). (7) New table cohorts (id, certification_id, week_start) and cohort_members (cohort_id, user_id, opted_in_at) for opt-in relatedness. (8) New table milestones (user_id, milestone_type, achieved_at) for unexpected non-contingent reward triggers. (9) Edge Function notification_copy_picker chooses templates from a curated set of autonomy-supportive copy variants. (10) Documentation in .claude/rules/copywriting.md codifying SDT-aligned voice for all admin-authored content.
risks: (1) DESIGN DISCIPLINE COST: enforcing SDT requires every copy decision and every reward decision to be reviewed against the framework - slower iteration than shipping a Duolingo-clone. Mitigation: codify rules in .claude/rules and do a one-time copy audit. (2) PERSONA-MARKET MISMATCH: some 20s persona users genuinely WANT competitive leaderboards (Achiever-strong); pure SDT-purism could feel under-stimulating. Mitigation: opt-in competition surfaces, default off. (3) JAPAN CULTURAL CONTEXT: longitudinal Japanese-school data shows autonomous motivation eroding under exam pressure - shikaq's autonomy framing might feel foreign vs the studyplus/受験サプリ 'try harder' tone the persona expects. Mitigation: validate copy with Japanese 20s women user testing. (4) OVERJUSTIFICATION TIGHTROPE: any premium plan benefit ('Super shikaq unlocks X') risks turning intrinsic interest into purchase-contingent extrinsic motivation. Mitigation: position premium as enabling competence (more practice depth) rather than gating basic learning. (5) MEASUREMENT DIFFICULTY: SDT outcomes (autonomy-satisfaction, competence-satisfaction) are not directly observable from product analytics - need periodic in-app surveys (Basic Psychological Needs in Learning Scale, BPNLS). (6) INSTITUTIONAL DRIFT: as the team grows and growth metrics dominate (DAU, conversion), SDT discipline will erode toward Duolingo-style controlling tactics. Mitigation: principle codified at CLAUDE.md / design-principles.md level so it is reviewed every PR.
apple_hig_compatibility: compatible. SDT and Apple HIG converge: HIG's Clarity/Deference/Less-is-more principles operationalize autonomy (don't dictate), competence (clear feedback), relatedness (caring tone). Apple's products explicitly avoid Skinner-box gamification (Apple Fitness rings reward effort INFORMATIONALLY, no chest reveals; Screen Time reports autonomously, doesn't lecture). SF Symbols' tone (calm, factual) and SF Pro's restraint match SDT's autonomy-supportive voice. Liquid Glass / semantic colors do not conflict. The only adaptation needed is to ensure shikaq's gamification additions (rings, milestones) are framed as informational mastery markers, not controlling levers - which is the same constraint HIG already imposes.
ethical_score: positive. SDT is the foundational framework cited by Center for Humane Technology, Time Well Spent, and Tristan Harris in their critiques of attention-capture design. SDT-aligned design produces low burnout, low time-regret, and high subjective well-being (Howard et al. 2021 meta-analysis). It directly counters dark-pattern monetization by surfacing the long-term cost of overjustification. The only ethical risk is SELECTIVE APPLICATION - using SDT vocabulary in marketing while shipping controlling mechanics underneath (greenwashing). Net: SDT raises shikaq's ethical floor and is a competitive differentiator vs Duolingo/StudySapuri's behaviorist tactics.
evidence_quality: rigorous_RCT + extensive meta-analysis. SDT has hundreds of randomized controlled trials across education, health, sport, workplace; multiple large meta-analyses (Deci, Koestner & Ryan 1999 reward studies; Howard et al. 2021 student outcomes 344 samples / 223k participants; Bureau et al. 2022 student motivation 144 studies / 79k students; Wang et al. 2024 SDT-based interventions). Among the most empirically supported motivation theories in psychology. Some constructs (Relative Autonomy Index) face psychometric criticism, and cross-cultural generalizability has been debated but generally upheld with refined operationalization.
localization_fit_japan: high (with calibration). Japanese 20s career-aspirant women experience high study-burnout under exam-prep apps that weaponize controlling extrinsic motivation (Studyplus streak pressure, online juku ranking). SDT-aligned design - autonomous pacing, informational feedback, caring tone - is a strong differentiator. Caveats: (1) Iyengar/Lepper-style critique - Japanese users may have higher tolerance for socially-prescribed goals (parent/teacher-defined), but Chirkov et al. show even in collectivist contexts, VOLITIONAL endorsement of those goals (= autonomy) drives well-being; the design implication is to respect users' chosen aspiration even if it is externally given. (2) Hayamizu & Otoshi longitudinal data shows autonomous motivation in Japanese schools eroded by exam pressure - shikaq has a market opening here as the FIRST autonomy-supportive certification app. (3) Relatedness in Japan often takes 仲間 (nakama) form - peer-with-shared-goal rather than head-to-head competition; map cohort/study-buddy features to that frame, not US-style leaderboards. Net: SDT is arguably MORE applicable to the Japanese 20s-female persona than to a US Achiever-Player audience.
